content: If we treat the Model Context Protocol as a universal 'plumbing standard' for AI Agent ecosystems, we can reframe AI agent building from the current practice of writing custom one-off tool integrations to assembling modular, interoperable agent components that work across any LLM backend. This shift would unlock a shared open marketplace of pre-built agent capabilities (from database querying to calendar scheduling) that any developer can drop into their agent without custom reconfiguration.
